{"version":"0","id":"aaaaaaaa-aaaa-1111-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaa10","detail-type":"DUMMY_FAILED_CHECK","source":"11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111__bbbbbbbb","account":"111111111111","time":"2024-12-09T09:16:51Z","region":"ab-abc-1","resources":[],"detail":{"id":"aaaaaaaa-aaaa-1111-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaa11","checkId":"allow-block-browsers","title":"Rare Dummy Activity","description":["Detects dummy users logging in from rare and unusual browsers that are on your blocklist, or not on your allow list.","Dummy browser activity can be a security issue due to the latency of applying patches to those browsers.","It may also indicate that a specific browser differs from what is typically utilized by the dummy end user, which could highlight dummy behavior that should be investigated.","Device prevalence is determined by looking at all successful events over 90 days across all connected integration instances, and automatically excludes any events where the browser, user agent or OS is 'Null' or 'Empty'. The 'Device prevalence threshold' setting uses a percentage of events coming from a particular browser to determine what is or isn't considered rare by the dummy setting.","The dummy check setting, which is enabled by default, will detect rare browsers if the device prevalence is less than 5 times in the last 7 days."],"recommendedActions":["Please review the activity of users tracked with a rare browser. Evaluate the user alongside other security anomalies like dummy locations or dummy login failures. You can exclude this user from the check or add the browser to your allowed
